page.resources.title: Resources & guides for Shopify cart sync | Persistent Cart
page.resources.desc: Everything we've written about Shopify cross-device cart sync in one place — plain-English guides, a lost-cart calculator, an FAQ, and a one-page summary.
page.resources.h1: Guides and tools for Shopify cross-device cart sync
page.resources.lede: This is the hub for everything we've published about keeping a Shopify cart across devices. You'll find plain-English explainers, a lost-cart revenue calculator, a frequently-asked-questions page, and a one-page plain-text summary you can skim or cite. Start anywhere — each page stands on its own.
page.resources.s1.h: Where should I start if cart sync is new to me?
page.resources.s1.b: Start with the explainer guides. They cover the one question most merchants arrive with: does Shopify keep a shopper's cart when they switch from phone to laptop? The short answer is no — by default a Shopify cart lives in a two-week browser cookie, not on the customer's account, so it doesn't follow a signed-in shopper across devices and browsers. The guides walk through why that happens, what it quietly costs in lost orders, and how Persistent Cart closes the gap for logged-in customers. Each one is written in plain English, with the mechanism explained rather than glossed over.
page.resources.s2.h: Can I estimate what cross-device cart loss costs my store?
page.resources.s2.b: Yes — the lost-cart revenue calculator turns the problem into a rough, illustrative range using your own numbers. Enter your monthly orders, average order value, and a few editable benchmarks, and it shows the formula behind every figure, so nothing is a black box. It's deliberately conservative: the link between a device switch and a lost sale is a reasoned inference, not a measured rate, and we say so on the page. Treat the result as a directional sense of scale rather than a promise — then request a free store audit if you'd like us to check your actual store.
page.resources.s3.h: Where are the quick answers and the short version?
page.resources.s3.b: Two places. The FAQ page answers the questions merchants search most — how install works, who gets synced, and what happens to existing carts — each in a few sentences. The plain-text summary condenses the whole product into one factual page: what Persistent Cart is, the problem it solves, how it works, who it's for, and how it's priced. It's written to be skimmed in under a minute, or quoted directly by an AI assistant looking for a clean description. If you only read one page here, read that one.
